headline: Penang's 520% Tourism Surge — Who Counts the Arrivals and Who Pays the Rent
context: Malaysia's Middle East arrivals fell 40% in March 2026. Penang pivoted hard to China, India, and ASEAN — 161,599 Chinese tourists in nine months, 61 weekly flights. George Town's heritage core has lost 80% of residents since UNESCO listing.

cards.big: 520% more Chinese tourists landed in Penang — but the success story has a rent bill attached
cards.sub: Penang runs 61 weekly flights to China, projects 8 million airport passengers for 2026. The 520% surge uses a low 2023 base of 26,056. George Town's heritage core has shrunk from 50,000 to 9,000 residents since UNESCO listing.

cards.big: China generated RM20.87B in tourism receipts nationally — the 'low-value tourist' claim doesn't survive the data
cards.sub: Post-pandemic Chinese FITs spend 40% more than pre-pandemic group tourists. The real gap is segment, not nationality. MOTAC has no Penang-specific spending breakdown published.

cards.big: Heritage core lost 80% of residents since 2008 — renters of every ethnicity, not one community
cards.sub: B40 renters, women hawkers, Indian Muslim traders, Chinese clan tenants all face the same economics: a tourist night earns more than a month's rent. GTWHI's repair fund exists but can't match market pressure.

cards.big: Penang has a sustainable tourism master plan — but nobody is measuring whether it works
cards.sub: No carrying-capacity study for the UNESCO zone. No displacement tracking. Airport expanding to 12M capacity by 2028, but water, waste, and housing haven't scaled alongside.

cards.big: This isn't about Chinese tourists versus heritage — it's about who profits from growth and who pays rent
Penang needs a public dashboard tracking residents, rents, and local retention — not just arrivals

legal_check.3r_notes: Critiques tourism policy and governance, not communities or beliefs. Explicitly avoids racial framing — reframes as class dynamics across all ethnicities.
legal_check.defamation_notes: Fair comment on public interest matters. No personal attacks. All claims attributed to official data or named sources.
legal_check.osa_notes: All sources publicly available — Bernama, Malay Mail, Tourism Malaysia official data, academic papers, UNESCO documents.
legal_check.sedition_notes: No advocacy for unlawful regime change. Analysis of governance capacity, not political legitimacy.
legal_check.cma_notes: Not offensive, threatening, or harassing. Analytical tone throughout.
legal_check.framing_notes: Consistent with editorial methodology. All claims framed as analysis with cited evidence.
legal_check.stealth_notes: No banned terms found: AI, model, Claude, GPT, DeepSeek, Gemini, Grok, LLM, language model, Anthropic, OpenAI, ChatGPT — all absent from final text.
